title: Long-context aggregate (RLM, as SQL)
description: Closes over the DETERMINISTIC HALF of RLM (Recursive Language Models, arXiv 2512.24601): once rows are labeled, the distributional query ('among instances for these user IDs, how many are label X') is one GROUP BY — the rows live as a DuckDB table, the agent never sees the whole context (no context rot), and the count is exact where RLM recurses and miscounts at long context. HONEST CAVEAT: the `label` is pre-given in the CSV here; the genuinely hard part RLM recurses on is INFERRING each label from free text — the semantic judgment boundary, shown as a map step in test/map-reduce-labeling.test.ts (worker labels partitions -> host single-writer merge -> aggregate). This manifest shows only the deterministic aggregate.

resolvers.title: DuckDB file scan
resolvers.description: Read a file into a table.
